danger_signals.visual_form: Dr. Robotnik as antagonist character in story presentation and opponent selection
danger_signals.meaning: Represents the primary threat to Beanville; signals evil intent through character design from Sonic franchise

danger_signals.visual_form: Narrative framing in game premise; opponents represent Robotnik's army
danger_signals.meaning: Danger comes from organized mechanical force kidnapping civilians; escalating threat through sequential opponents

danger_signals.visual_form: Game setting description where bean creatures are imprisoned
danger_signals.meaning: Oppressive, dangerous environments where captives are held before transformation

danger_signals.visual_form: Conceptual device described in narrative; represents transformation threat
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate danger: loss of identity and freedom through forced mechanization

danger_signals.visual_form: Grey colored beans appearing on player grid during puzzle gameplay
danger_signals.meaning: Mechanical hindrance in puzzle; cannot be matched normally; represent obstacles spawned by opponent success

danger_signals.visual_form: Beans spilling over top of grid
danger_signals.meaning: Loss state; visual indicator of failure and overwhelm by the threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Player character representing Carbuncle from Puyo Puyo; makes different animations based on play quality
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and hope; character responds positively to good performance, reinforcing player empowerment

safety_signals.visual_form: Narrative goal: breaking into dungeons to rescue captives
safety_signals.meaning: Liberation and restoration of freedom; puzzle victories = rescue missions

safety_signals.visual_form: Four same-colored beans vanishing when grouped; visual feedback of successful action
safety_signals.meaning: Progress and control; player agency directly clearing threats from board

safety_signals.visual_form: Sequential matches triggered by single action; cascading bean drops
safety_signals.meaning: Momentum and success; growing power through strategic play; visual feedback of mastery

safety_signals.visual_form: 13 increasingly challenging opponents in Scenario Mode
safety_signals.meaning: Structured challenge growth signaling gradual empowerment; player defeats progressively stronger foes

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dr. Robotnik character design; mechanical aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: 13 different opponents in Scenario Mode representing Robotnik's army; characters drawn from Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog animated series
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dark dungeons as Robotnik-controlled spaces; the Mean Bean-Steaming Machine as faction technology/infrastructure

faction_indicators.color_identity: Multicolored beans (matching-based gameplay uses multiple colors)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Has Bean (Carbuncle); bean-like creatures as population identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Beanville as the free/safe location; puzzle grid represents contested space; successful matches = rescued citizens

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game scenario setup; opponents presented as Robotnik's forces; dark dungeons and steaming machine in narrative
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents play puzzle matches against player; refugee beans spawn to hinder player progress; escalating difficulty shows growing threat intensity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Has Bean character animations change based on performance quality; opponents defeated one-by-one in Scenario Mode progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cleared beans disappear; chain combos trigger; refugee beans sent to opponent; visual momentum builds with successful plays

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Beans spill over top of grid; loss condition reached
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Game ends; dungeon remains unbreached; rescue mission fails

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Successful completion of Scenario Mode progression; all 13 opponents defeated
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Beanville is freed; bean creatures rescued from dungeons and steaming machine; threat eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: 13 opponents in Scenario Mode, each representing members of Robotnik's forces; player must defeat each sequentially to progress through the 'dungeon rescue' narrative
Matching and clearing beans represents freeing captive creatures; refugee beans (grey, unmatchable) represent the mechanical oppression Robotnik imposes
Has Bean's animation changes reflect play quality, creating character responsiveness that mirrors player agency in the liberation narrative
Dark dungeons and Mean Bean-Steaming Machine named in premise to establish oppressive setting; Beanville as contrasting free world
13 increasingly challenging opponents signal rising difficulty in rescue mission; each victory increments progress toward final liberation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Distinct positive animations; character responds encouragingly to player success
Different animations indicating struggle; character responds to difficulty
Sends refugee beans to player grid via chain combos; increases board pressure as player fails to clear efficiently
Cannot be matched directly; only removed by adjacent matches; persist and accumulate, increasing board clutter and loss risk

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Four or more same color aligned
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Instantly disappear; trigger cascade (beans above drop); potential chain reactions; send refugees to opponent
Increasing visual warning of loss imminent; overflow triggers immediate loss condition

notes: Dr. Robotnik's Mean Bean Machine uses the Sonic franchise IP overlay on Puyo Puyo mechanics to create a thematic puzzle experience. The world-state is binary: Robotnik's occupation (before defeating all 13 opponents) vs. Beanville's liberation (scenario victory). Visual communication relies heavily on narrative framing and character roster rather than environmental design, as the core gameplay is puzzle-based on an abstract grid. Danger is communicated through the kidnapping/transformation narrative and through gameplay mechanics (refugee beans, overflow threat). Safety comes from player agency in clearing matches and progressing through opponents. The game's visual manifestation of political conflict is entirely narrative-driven; the puzzle grid itself remains abstract and colorless except for the falling beans.
